title.en: Cafe Etiquette: Asking for a seat and watching your bags
title.ko: 여기 자리 있어요? (카페에서 자리 확인하고 짐 부탁하기)

description.en: Is this seat taken? Learn how to check for availability and the polite way to ask someone to watch your stuff in a busy Korean cafe.
description.ko: 빈자리가 있는지 묻는 법부터, 잠시 화장실에 갈 때 옆 사람에게 짐을 부탁하는 리액션까지! 한국 카페 이용의 꿀팁을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Life. Cafe. Study. Request. Seat. Manner. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.emotion: 노트북 가방을 멘 채 주변을 살피는 목소리로 시작
content.text: Mina, I’m at this really popular cafe to do some work, but it’s packed!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I see a chair, but there’s a jacket on the table. How do I ask if it's okay to sit there?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 주인 없는 자리인지 꼭 확인해야 하죠.
content.translation: In that case, you must check if it's a seat without an owner.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 한국 사람들은 이럴 때..., "여기 자리 있어요?" 라고 물어봐요.
content.translation: Koreans ask "Yeogiseo jari isseoyo?" in these situations.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wait, doesn't that literally mean "Is there a seat here?"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 하지만 속뜻은..., "이 자리에 주인 있나요?" 또는..., "앉아도 될까요?" 라는 뜻이에요.
content.translation: Right. But the inner meaning is "Is there an owner for this seat?" or "May I sit here?"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 카페에서 자리를 잡고, 잠시 짐을 부탁하는 리얼한 리액션들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn real reactions for securing a seat and asking to watch your stuff at a cafe!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 실례합니다, 여기 자리 있어요?

content.speaker: John
content.text: This is a very safe way to start, right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 만약 주인이 있으면 "네 있어요" 라고 할 거고 없으면 "아뇨 없어요" 라고 할 거예요.
content.translation: Yes! If there's an owner, they'll say "Yes, there is," and if not, they'll say "No, there isn't."

insight.title: 여기 자리 있어요?
insight.usage_tip: 여기(Here) + 자리(Seat) + 있어요?(Is there?). Even if there's no person, if there's a bag or jacket, it's polite to ask the person at the next table.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, and what if I need to go to the restroom but I don't want to pack up everything?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 옆 사람에게..., "짐 좀 봐주시겠어요?" 라고 정중하게 부탁해 보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try asking the person next to you politely, "Jim jom bwajusigesseoyo?"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 한국에서는 아주 흔한 풍경이지만, 이렇게 리액션을 하면 훨씬 더 안전하고 마음이 편하죠.
content.translation: It's a very common sight in Korea, but reacting like this makes it much safer and more comfortable.

insight.title: 짐 좀 봐주시겠어요?
insight.usage_tip: 짐(Stuff/Bags) + 좀(Please/A bit) + 봐주시겠어요?(Could you look at/watch for me?). Use this when you're stepping away for a short moment, like a restroom break or to pick up your drink.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I found a nice spot next to Mina, but I need to make sure the chair next to her is free.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저기요 실례합니다. 혹시 여기 자리 있어요?
content.translation: Excuse me, sorry to bother you. Is this seat taken by any chance?

insight.title: 혹시
insight.usage_tip: Adding 'Hoksi' at the beginning makes the question sound much softer and less intrusive.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아뇨. 자리 없어요. 앉으세요.
content.translation: Ah, no. The seat is free. Please sit down.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아! 감사합니다.
content.translation: Ah, thank you.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저기 죄송한데, 짐 좀 봐주시겠어요? 화장실 좀 다녀올게요.
content.translation: Excuse me, I'm sorry, but could you watch my stuff? I'll be right back from the restroom.

insight.title: 화장실 좀 다녀올게요
insight.description: 'I'll go to the restroom and come back'
insight.usage_tip: Giving a reason (Restroom) for why you are leaving your stuff helps the other person understand the situation.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네 걱정 마세요. 제가 보고 있을게요!
content.translation: Yes, don't worry. I'll be watching!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 봐주셔서 감사합니다! 덕분에 잘 다녀왔어요.
content.translation: Thank you for watching! Thanks to you, I went and came back well.

content.speaker: John
content.text: It’s amazing how people are so willing to watch your expensive laptop!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. "짐 좀 봐주세요" 라는 리액션 하나가 서로에 대한 신뢰를 보여주는 한국만의 독특한 문화죠.
content.translation: Right. The reaction "Please watch my stuff" is a unique Korean culture that shows mutual trust.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "cafe manners" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 여기 자리 있어요?
content.translation: Is this seat taken?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 여기 앉아도 될까요?
content.translation: May I sit here?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 짐 좀 봐주시겠어요?
content.translation: Could you watch my stuff for a second?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 금방 다녀올게요!
content.translation: I'll be back in a second!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 봐주셔서 정말 감사합니다!
content.translation: Thank you so much for watching!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 카페에서 마음에 드는 자리를 발견한다면, 웃으면서 먼저 물어보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, if you find a seat you like at a cafe today, try asking with a smile first.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "여기 자리 있어요?"
content.translation: Is this seat taken?

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 주말에는 카페에 빈자리가 없어서 한참 기다렸어요. (Since it's the weekend, there were no empty seats in the cafe, so I waited for a while.)
여행을 가기 전에 짐을 꼼꼼하게 챙겨야 해요. (You should pack your stuff carefully before going on a trip.)
동생이 숙제를 다 할 때까지 엄마가 옆에서 봐주셨어요. (Mom looked after my younger sibling until they finished their homework.)
퇴근 시간 지하철은 항상 사람들로 꽉 차 있어요. (The subway during rush hour is always packed with people.)
친구 사이에는 신뢰를 지키는 것이 가장 중요해요. (The most important thing between friends is keeping trust.)
잠깐 편의점에 다녀올 테니까 여기서 기다려요. (I'll go to the convenience store for a moment and come back, so wait here.)
